rationale: Ground truth is thin and mostly social (blurred-image question, LGTM, a startup-success confirmation) plus one inline ask about whether the test's reduce_scatter hasattr checks are sufficient; neither candidate reproduces that concern, but Y at least explicitly engages the same test lines (121-126) while X never mentions reduce_scatter testing at all, giving Y marginally better recall. Both candidates' own findings are plausible and diff-grounded, but X leans on an unverified claim about destroy_model_parallel behavior from a file not in the diff, whereas Y stays strictly within the shown diff and offers two concrete, actionable suggestions (add a branch test for the dp_group-None/world_size<=1 guard with specifics on what to mock/assert, and add an inline comment justifying the cfg_parallel_size guard removal). X's single suggested comment is vaguer ('worth a one-line comment'). Overall Y is slightly stronger on grounding and actionability.
